titleStmt.title: Letter to the Resident Medical Superintendent, Killarney, 3 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter to the Residential Medical Superintendent of the Killarney District Asylum. The letter regards the appointment of a new dressmaker and nurse at the asylum. It states that the author was directed to forward on the communications received from Mary O'Leary (1894-?), a dress maker from Kerry, for any observations the board may wish to make.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3755

correspAction.placeName: District Asylum, Killarney, County Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
3rd February, 1916
252 Sir, I am directed by the Inspectors to forward, for
any observations the Committee may wish to make, the
accompanying copy of a communication received from
Miss Mary O'Leary regarding the appointment of Dress-
maker and Nurse in the asylum. I am,
Sir,
Your Obedient Servant, The Resident Medical Superintendent
District Asylum
KILLARNEY
